channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How a stranger helped a man falling on the subway without damaging his dignity
item.description: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.
item.content:encoded: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.

item.title: Here to Help: Jennifer Timmick guides visitors through the stories of American history
item.description: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.content:encoded: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: The Gambler 500 rally combines trash pickup and old cars
item.description: For the "Gambler 500," people gather on forest service roads in Oregon for a colossal trash pick-up on national forest land, conducted in festive and junky old cars.
item.content:encoded: For the "Gambler 500," people gather on forest service roads in Oregon for a colossal trash pick-up on national forest land, conducted in festive and junky old cars.
item.dc:creator: Matt Martin

item.title: They were world-class tennis rivals. Now friends, they've teamed up against cancer
item.description: They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set.
item.content:encoded:
They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set.

(Image credit: Trevor Jones)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: An assistant district attorney and a madam join forces in 'A Pair of Aces'
item.description: Marie Benedict and Victoria Christopher Murray bring readers a new historical novel that presents two women on opposite ends of the law who must partner to bring down a crime boss in 1930s New York.
item.content:encoded: Marie Benedict and Victoria Christopher Murray bring readers a new historical novel that presents two women on opposite ends of the law who must partner to bring down a crime boss in 1930s New York.
item.dc:creator: Keishel Williams

item.title: 3 World Cup rivals find 'Common Ground' in a cross-border beer
item.description: A trio of craft breweries from the global soccer event's three host nations are using the tournament to brew something increasingly rare: cross-border solidarity.
item.content:encoded:
A trio of craft breweries from the global soccer event's three host nations are using the tournament to brew something increasingly rare: cross-border solidarity.

(Image credit: Justin Gellerson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Trump's name is off Kennedy Center, but he's still center stage as Maher wins Twain prize
item.description: Comedian Bill Maher received The Mark Twain Prize for American Humor on Sunday night at the troubled Kennedy Center, where a tarp continues to cover the forced removal of President Trump's name.
item.content:encoded:
Comedian Bill Maher received The Mark Twain Prize for American Humor on Sunday night at the troubled Kennedy Center, where a tarp continues to cover the forced removal of President Trump's name.

(Image credit: Clifton Prescod)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: 'House of the Dragon,' Season 3, Episode 2: Honey, I'm home!
item.description: In which Rhaenyra goes from grief-stricken in bed to sword-swinging in the Red Keep.
item.content:encoded:
In which Rhaenyra goes from grief-stricken in bed to sword-swinging in the Red Keep.

(Image credit: Ollie Upton)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Milwaukee's Schlitz Beer says goodbye after 177 years
item.description: Pabst Brewing has stopped producing Schlitz beer. Schlitz is known as "the beer that made Milwaukee famous" and has a 177-year history.
item.content:encoded: Pabst Brewing has stopped producing Schlitz beer. Schlitz is known as "the beer that made Milwaukee famous" and has a 177-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: That's HOT!
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KNOW listener Dan Sadoff of St. Paul, Minnesota along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KNOW listener Dan Sadoff of St. Paul, Minnesota along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz
